title: Cohere releases North Mini Code, an open model for agentic software engineering
friendlyTitle: Cohere releases an open coding model for agent workflows
summary: Cohere released North Mini Code, a 30B-parameter sparse MoE model with 3B active parameters under Apache 2.0. The model is designed for agentic coding, terminal workflows, and software engineering tasks across multiple agent scaffolds.
friendlySummary: North Mini Code is an open model built for coding agents, terminal work, and software engineering tasks. It is available through Hugging Face, OpenCode, and Cohere’s API.
whyItMatters: Coding models are increasingly optimized for tool use and full agent loops, not just code-completion benchmarks. More capable open models give developers and companies more options for private, inspectable, and cost-controlled coding workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Developers get another open option for coding assistants. That matters for teams that want more control over cost, privacy, and how their coding tools work.
